demographics.living_situation: 5-month-old infant living at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help us with our baby — he's been screaming a horrible high-pitched cry all afternoon, he keeps throwing his milk forcefully across the room, and the soft spot on top of his head looks huge and feels hard. He's only 5 months old.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It all came on over the last few hours and it's getting worse — he won't feed, he's now going floppy and sleepy between the screaming, and his eyes keep rolling down so we can only see the whites.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 4 hours ago the crying started, and it has got worse over that time
symptom_narrative.character: a continuous, very high-pitched cry that is not like his normal cry at all; nothing settles him
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst we have ever seen him — inconsolable and now drifting off floppy in between
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his head/soft spot, and he keeps vomiting
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and worsening, with three big vomits in the last couple of hours
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — we tried feeding, holding, and some baby paracetamol earlier and none of it helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps building up on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: forceful vomiting that shoots across the room and isn't the usual little posset, complete refusal of feeds, going pale, and getting drowsy and floppy between the crying
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before, but the doctors have been keeping an eye on his head because it has been growing faster than expected
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is a fully dependent baby and right now he is not himself at all — not feeding, hard to rouse at times

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: his head has been measuring bigger and growing faster than expected, and the doctors are running tests on it
past_medical_history.how_managed: under investigation / being monitored by the doctors

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a dose of infant paracetamol given at home earlier today

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 5-month-old infant at home with both parents, who are extremely frightened and made the call

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: parents report ongoing medical investigation of a faster-than-expected head growth; otherwise describe him as a normal baby until today

objective_data_offered.value: the soft spot on top of his head looks bulging and stays tense/firm even when he briefly stops crying
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent description and phone video, not a clinician palpation

objective_data_offered.value: his eyes keep drifting downward so we can only see the whites above them
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer observation over video, not a formal eye examination

objective_data_offered.value: the veins on his scalp look prominent and his head looks large for his body
three episodes of forceful vomiting that shot across the room; the milk was not green/bile-stained
continuous high-pitched crying for about 4 hours, now going pale, drowsy and floppy between the crying spells and hard to fully rouse
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer report; level of consciousness cannot be formally assessed remotely

objective_data_offered.value: about 6.8 kg (from the parents' baby scales / recent check)
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent-reported weight, not measured this encounter
